evidence: The current audit matches file, symbol, occurrence, and rule, detects unused exceptions as stale, and focused tests cover exact admission, one-occurrence isolation, and duplicate rejection.
The registry has repeated exact occurrence entries for one Upstash facade; its current representation is verbose but preserves the relevant identity.
rejectedAlternatives: Simplify to a symbol-only or file-only exception registry.
Replace the registry with a semantic/generated representation before it demonstrably preserves exact, duplicate, occurrence, and stale detection.
acceptedOrDefaultBehavior: Retain the occurrence-based registry as the default. HGI-302/HGI-306 may propose a semantic/generated representation only with equivalent positive and negative fixtures proving exact match, duplicate rejection, occurrence isolation, and stale-entry detection against the real audit.

taskBlocking.inheritedNonClaim: No replacement boundary control may land without equivalent negative fixtures.
Control admission/retirement must preserve current exact, duplicate, occurrence, and stale detection.

authorityImpact: This is a repository-local control decision and grants no provider or workflow authority.
nonClaims: No semantic/generated replacement is implemented or proven equivalent.
Registry line-count reduction is not a success criterion.
carryingCost: Maintain occurrence entries and their fixtures; review only when a repeated exception pattern has a candidate canonical owner and comparison evidence.
reviewOrResumeTrigger: Resume when HGI-302 or HGI-306 supplies a candidate representation plus real-audit fixture comparison covering all four preserved properties.
